items.title: Arrays & Lists
items.description: Both patterns rely on O(1) indexed access — the natural fit for arrays/lists.

items.title: Strings
items.description: Substring / token-level scans (longest unique, anagram windows) are classic sliding-window territory.

items.title: Linked Lists
items.description: Fast/slow two-pointer (Floyd's tortoise-and-hare) detects cycles and finds midpoints in linked lists.

items.title: Sorting Algorithms
items.description: Sorting first turns 'find a pair with target sum' from O(n²) into O(n log n) sort + O(n) two-pointer.

items.title: Big-O Complexity
items.description: Both patterns are O(n) replacements for O(n²) double loops — textbook complexity reductions.

items.title: Problem-Solving Patterns
items.description: Two-pointer and sliding-window are two of the most common interview problem patterns.
